title: Lisa The Drama Queen

jokes.text: Worst couch gag ever~
jokes.setup: Traditional Simpsons opening couch gag sequence
jokes.punchline: Direct acknowledgment that this particular couch gag was terrible
jokes.explanation: Self-aware meta-humor about the show's own production quality. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: You're older. Why don't you do these fun activities? Uh, cause no one's making me.
jokes.setup: Homer telling Bart he'll miss these activities when older
jokes.punchline: Bart's logical comeback exposing adult hypocrisy about 'fun' activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect child logic cutting through adult nonsense. Great character voice for Bart.

jokes.text: Ten thousand years of knowledge will be passed on to those whose parents have signed the permission slip. Rest of you have just purchased very expensive pajamas.
jokes.setup: Grandiose introduction about ancient martial arts wisdom
jokes.punchline: Bureaucratic reality of permission slips and overpriced martial arts uniforms
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast between mystical martial arts and mundane parenting bureaucracy.

jokes.text: Pardon me, master. But what does a big fat wad like you know about Kungfu?
jokes.setup: Respectful address to martial arts master
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt insult about the instructor's physique
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer tactlessness. Character-appropriate but predictable fat joke.

jokes.text: Kungfu is not about phisique. But the channelling of ones energy or Qi. If you doubt my skill, please feel free to attack at my signal.
jokes.setup: Dignified explanation of martial arts philosophy, invitation to test his skill
jokes.punchline: Sets up Homer's inevitable painful lesson
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for physical comedy payoff. Functional but unremarkable.

jokes.text: Ooh! My Qi!
jokes.setup: Homer gets physically defeated by kung fu master
jokes.punchline: Homer's pained cry using the mystical term he just learned
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to setup. Homer adopting mystical terminology for his pain is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Do we have any more paints? My orange isn't the orange of that orange.
jokes.setup: Art class painting exercise
jokes.punchline: Lisa's perfectionist approach to matching orange colors
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's attention to detail and perfectionism.

jokes.text: Just paint the damn fruit. Lower your expectation, Lisa.
jokes.setup: Lisa's artistic perfectionism
jokes.punchline: Instructor's blunt dismissal of artistic standards
jokes.explanation: Realistic teacher frustration with overly ambitious student.

jokes.text: I was painting with my heart. And if you look carefully, you can see that the knight is Josh Groban, our young centry's greatest vocalist.
jokes.setup: Defense of artistic choices
jokes.punchline: Revealing the knight in medieval painting is actually Josh Groban
jokes.explanation: Establishes Juliet's Josh Groban obsession through absurd artistic interpretation.

jokes.text: Oh! Another Grobanite.
jokes.setup: Juliet's Josh Groban reference
jokes.punchline: Teacher's weary recognition of Josh Groban fans as a known type
jokes.explanation: Good economy. Creates the sense that Josh Groban fans are a recognizable plague on art teachers.

jokes.text: I do not need this! My family owns a gas station.
jokes.setup: Art teacher facing student rebellion
jokes.punchline: Random assertion about family gas station ownership as if it's relevant
jokes.explanation: Absurd non-sequitur suggesting the art teacher has other career options. Random but memorable.

jokes.text: What are you staring at? Everyone paint me 20 laps.
jokes.setup: Art teacher trying to regain control of class
jokes.punchline: Mixing up 'paint' and 'run' - ordering students to 'paint laps'
jokes.explanation: Excellent malapropism combining art class with gym class punishment. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I need some candy for our first play date, but I don't wanna come on too strong. M&Ms? Well, if i pick plain, she'll think I'm cheap. But if I pick peanut, she may have an allergy. You just killed her, Bart!
jokes.setup: Lisa overthinking candy selection for friend
jokes.punchline: Escalating to 'you just killed her' over peanut allergy concern
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa anxiety spiral. The 'you just killed her' escalation is character-perfect overthinking.

jokes.text: You asked for my help, then you don't want it. Excuse me, but why not consider a Norman Joy? It looks like you only bought something for yourself. But then you just have fun to have two pieces.
jokes.setup: Bart frustrated with Lisa's candy indecision, clerk offers suggestion
jokes.punchline: Clerk's overly detailed candy bar psychology and sharing strategy
jokes.explanation: Overly analytical candy bar marketing wisdom. Decent observational humor about candy psychology.

jokes.text: Finally, a real suggestion. If she doesn't like coconut, you're screwed!
jokes.setup: Lisa grateful for clerk's candy advice
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately pointing out the coconut allergy flaw
jokes.explanation: Solid callback to the allergy concerns. Bart deflating Lisa's relief is good sibling dynamic.

jokes.text: Hi, juliet. -So what do you want to do? I don't know. It's been so long since i've had a play date. Not that i couldn't get one if i wanted, I...
jokes.setup: Lisa meeting Juliet for their playdate
jokes.punchline: Lisa's awkward over-explanation about her social life
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Lisa's social awkwardness and defensive nature about her limited friendships.

jokes.text: My parents bought me a membership card to the fake art museum. Does it get you into special exhibits? On Wednesdays. -That's today!
jokes.setup: Lisa mentioning museum membership
jokes.punchline: 'Fake art museum' - implying it's a museum of fake art, not a fake museum
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay - 'fake art museum' could mean museum of forgeries or fake museum. Good misdirection.

jokes.text: Won't last.
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on Lisa finally having a friend
jokes.punchline: Bart's cynical two-word dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart cynicism with maximum economy. Two words perfectly capture his dismissive attitude.

jokes.text: We could be murderers. -Could have been if we hadn't had kids.
jokes.setup: Marge pointing out they could be dangerous strangers
jokes.punchline: Homer's dark implication that kids prevented their murder career
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor. Homer casually suggesting parenthood was the only thing stopping their murder spree.

jokes.text: Told you, Marge. Told ya!
jokes.setup: Juliet's father proving legal system is the real villain
jokes.punchline: Homer vindicated in some previous argument about legal system
jokes.explanation: Homer's petty vindication over being right about something. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Equalia~where everyone is equal, but we're in charge. I mean somebody has to be, right?
jokes.setup: Creating an egalitarian fantasy kingdom
jokes.punchline: Immediately establishing their own superiority within the 'equal' system
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about how even egalitarian movements create hierarchies. Smart kids' logic.

jokes.text: Lisa, do you want to be best friends? Gosh, this is all happening so fast, I don't know what to say. Say yes! Say yes!
jokes.setup: Juliet asking about friendship status
jokes.punchline: Lisa treating friendship like a marriage proposal, then coaching herself
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy about the awkwardness of formalizing childhood friendships.

jokes.text: You heard me say that, didn't you? -I did. We're still cool, alright? -We are. My best friend is so cool.
jokes.setup: Lisa accidentally saying her thoughts out loud
jokes.punchline: Awkward acknowledgment followed by continued awkward enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe moment about social awkwardness in new friendships.

jokes.text: Friendship is like marriage. The key is listening. Also, if her dog bites you... don't make a big deal out of it. -I just said that. Ok, honey. If it is that important to you, you said it.
jokes.setup: Marge giving friendship advice to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Homer repeating the dog bite advice, then patronizingly letting Marge 'win'
jokes.explanation: Good married couple dynamic. Homer's patronizing 'you said it' response is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: A twonicorn~
jokes.setup: Need for a fantasy creature with two horns
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'two' and 'unicorn' creating 'twonicorn'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective portmanteau. Gets funnier with Juliet's accent emphasis.

jokes.text: Girls are so lame. Isn't that right, headless starvader? What's that? You missed your girl friend armless Malibu Stacey wrapped in hockey tape?
jokes.setup: Bart dismissing girls as lame
jokes.punchline: Immediately talking to his own makeshift 'girlfriend' doll
jokes.explanation: Excellent hypocrisy reveal. Bart's sad doll relationship immediately undercuts his criticism of girls.

jokes.text: Use as many big words as possible. I call computer. Bart, you can have doorbell.
jokes.setup: Family preparing to impress classy dinner guests
jokes.punchline: Homer and Bart dividing up the 'big words' they'll use
jokes.explanation: Good insight into how working-class family prepares for 'fancy' people. Homer and Bart's word division is perfectly stupid.

jokes.text: Nice doorbell, is it computerized? Hey, that wha... Homer. -Uh..Uh...we...I You come up with an excuse.
jokes.setup: Bart using his assigned 'big word' about the doorbell
jokes.punchline: Homer completely failing to use 'computer' and giving up
jokes.explanation: Good callback payoff. Homer's immediate failure and surrender is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Huh huh...if you say so my lord.
jokes.setup: Trying to impress the academic dinner guest
jokes.punchline: Homer's awkward overly formal address
jokes.explanation: Homer's class anxiety manifesting as medieval deference. Mildly funny social awkwardness.

jokes.text: If you show me to your bath room, I'd like to pretend to wash my hands.
jokes.setup: Polite request to use facilities
jokes.punchline: Honest admission that he's only 'pretending' to wash hands
jokes.explanation: Excellent honesty about social pretenses. The casual admission of fake hand-washing is perfectly deadpan.

jokes.text: When they make a movie for this book, does *** free tickets?
jokes.setup: Academic discussing John Grisham scholarship
jokes.punchline: Homer's only interest is potential movie tickets
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response to intellectual discussion - immediately focuses on personal benefit.

jokes.text: I hate track three. I hate it! I hate it!(sobbing)
jokes.setup: Father playing John Grisham soundtrack
jokes.punchline: Juliet's intense emotional breakdown over specific soundtrack track
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Juliet's emotional instability. Specific hatred of 'track three' is absurdly detailed.

jokes.text: Mmm, that's chewing gum.
jokes.setup: Homer asking doctor to look at lump on his back
jokes.punchline: Casual diagnosis that it's just gum
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery. The casual medical diagnosis of gum is both gross and funny.

jokes.text: What a racket!
jokes.setup: Doctor correctly diagnosing his 'medical' issue
jokes.punchline: Homer treating the ten-dollar consultation as a scam despite getting accurate diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - complaining about effective service. Shows his contrarian nature.

jokes.text: I'm a giraffopus, I'm uncomfortable in water and on land.
jokes.setup: Fantasy sequence with made-up creature
jokes.punchline: Creature that combines giraffe and octopus, uncomfortable in both habitats
jokes.explanation: Creative creature concept. The habitat complaint is a logical consequence of the absurd hybrid.

jokes.text: Mom, please don't do that. Who is this mom of which you speak? Mom, stop! This is our thing, not yours. Um, I'm just trying to... It's better if you don't.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to participate in girls' fantasy play
jokes.punchline: Lisa's mortified rejection of parental intrusion into friend activity
jokes.explanation: Realistic childhood embarrassment about parents trying to join in. Good social cringe.

jokes.text: I search all the children's facebook pages for unflattering references to me.
jokes.setup: Skinner mentioning knowing about Lisa's Facebook
jokes.punchline: Revealing he stalks students' social media for criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner paranoia and social media creeping. Character-perfect and topically relevant.

jokes.text: By the way, I enjoy the photos of your trip to your sanity. Huh huh that. We were actually just going out for brunch and I get lost.
jokes.setup: Skinner making joke about trip to 'insanity'
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal explanation of getting lost going to brunch
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection. Homer's mundane explanation undermines Skinner's clever wordplay joke.

jokes.text: I'm Bart Simpson's mother. You think you've got any tricks I haven't seen?
jokes.setup: Lisa threatening to disobey
jokes.punchline: Marge's veteran parent confidence based on Bart experience
jokes.explanation: Excellent Marge line. Having survived Bart, Lisa's rebellion seems trivial. Great family dynamic.

jokes.text: I'd love to! Oh, but I'm supposed to attend the model UN this weekend. I'm the delegate from Azerbaijan, and I'm threatening a rice tariff.
jokes.setup: Lisa wanting to run away with Juliet
jokes.punchline: Lisa's serious commitment to model UN roleplay about rice tariffs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa nerdiness. The specificity of Azerbaijan and rice tariffs makes this exceptionally quotable.

jokes.text: Name. -Dr. William McDougle. From now on, you're ground keeper Willy. Next.
jokes.setup: Equality registration scene
jokes.punchline: Willie's academic credentials ignored, assigned to his actual job
jokes.explanation: Good irony about Willie having hidden credentials but being assigned his obvious role.

jokes.text: I wonder if Lisa has threaten the model UN with the rice tariff yet. I doubt it. Friday is usually just comedy assignments and procedure old rules.
jokes.setup: Parents wondering about Lisa's whereabouts
jokes.punchline: Marge's detailed knowledge of model UN scheduling
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier joke. Marge's familiarity with model UN procedures shows her involvement in Lisa's interests.

jokes.text: Total chaos. We're two minutes away from a roll call vote on the nonbinding resolution, and no Azerbaijan!
jokes.setup: Asking about Lisa's attendance at model UN
jokes.punchline: Martin's dramatic description of model UN 'crisis' without Azerbaijan
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of nerd drama. Martin treating model UN absence as international crisis is perfectly character-appropriate.

jokes.text: To the extent you can trust the word of a Belgian, yes!
jokes.setup: Confirming Lisa's absence from model UN
jokes.punchline: Martin's inexplicable distrust of Belgians in model UN context
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly random geopolitical prejudice. The specificity of Belgian distrust is absurdly perfect.

jokes.text: What about a jacket and jeans with a nice T-shirt? Just go!
jokes.setup: Homer asking about appropriate dress for rescue mission
jokes.punchline: Continuing to overthink outfit during emergency
jokes.explanation: Good Homer priority confusion. Focusing on attire during child rescue mission is perfectly obtuse.

jokes.text: You know what I think killed this place. No one wanted to drive to the middle of the forest for clams.
jokes.setup: Arriving at the abandoned restaurant
jokes.punchline: Homer's obvious business analysis of the location failure
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about obviously poor restaurant location. Homer stating the obvious with confidence.

jokes.text: This place is where we came after my bar mitzvah. You said you weren't having one. Uh, it was just family.
jokes.setup: Bullies recognizing the restaurant location
jokes.punchline: Awkward admission about secret family bar mitzvah
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing bully's hidden Jewish identity and family shame.

jokes.text: At least my dad didn't smoke after his stroke.
jokes.setup: Being called 'buttbrain'
jokes.punchline: Dolph's dark comeback about family medical trauma
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly dark bully comeback. The specificity of 'smoking after stroke' makes it memorably harsh.

jokes.text: I like those ones. Tell me about those.
jokes.setup: Juliet describing various dragons
jokes.punchline: Tough bully genuinely interested in 'chubby bold dragons'
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of bully stereotype. Tough kid being drawn into fantasy story shows universal appeal.

jokes.text: An ambitious first novel by the two brightest young writers this side of the Iowa workshop. I bet it'll burn up good!
jokes.setup: Dolph seeming to praise the girls' writing
jokes.punchline: Reveals intention to literally burn the book
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection where literary appreciation becomes threat of destruction

jokes.text: Re-jected!
jokes.setup: Lisa receives formal rejection letter from publisher
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt, enthusiastic pronunciation of rejection
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer insensitivity delivered with characteristic oblivious enthusiasm

jokes.text: You and your friend, princess nutjob inspired me to write my own fantasy novel based on my experiences as a dad.
jokes.setup: Homer consoling Lisa about her writing rejection
jokes.punchline: Homer's decision to write his own fantasy novel about parenting
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Homer's writing attempt. 'Princess nutjob' is a perfectly Homer-esque description.

jokes.text: Oh~Writing is hard!
jokes.setup: Homer attempting to write his fantasy novel
jokes.punchline: Immediate defeat after minimal effort
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment. Immediate surrender after attempting something difficult. Great economy and character truth.

tmdb_overview: Lisa lets her imagination run wild when she makes a new friend and they create a magical kingdom together called Equalia, complete with characters and plotlines. But they find themselves facing a nightmare when the boundaries between reality and fantasy are blurred. With the voice of Emily Blunt.
